zh.lifeSummary: 當John James Ellithorpe Watson於1472年6月出生在英國越可爾霍爾--斯帕爾丁-姆爾時，他的父親Daniel Watson22歲，母親Matilda Harriet Henderson23歲。他於1495年與Agnes Young在英國英格蘭越可爾克利夫蘭結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1521年3月11日在英國越可爾勒夫 泊享年48歲，埋葬在英國越可爾霍爾--斯帕爾丁-姆爾。

nameMeanings.name: Watson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish, English, and Irish: patronymic meaning ‘son of Wat’, a pet form of Walter that was particularly common in Scotland and northern England. See Watt .

variants.name: Walter
meanings: English (of Norman origin), German, Dutch, Polish, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This personal name was introduced into England both in the reign of Edward the Confessor and by the Normans (in the forms Walt(i)er, Waut(i)er). After the Norman Conquest it soon became one of the most popular personal names in Britain. Compare Walther .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Valter and Czech Valtr: from the personal name Valter, Valtr, of ancient Germanic origin (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

nameMeanings.name: Ellithorpe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Allerthorpe (Yorkshire). This surname is no longer found in Britain.

History: It was Thomas Ellithorpe from Holme upon Spalding Moor in Yorkshire, England, who brought this surname to MA in the middle of the 17th century. In Britain the name is also recorded as Ellethorpe and Elthorpe, the latter form being the only one that is still in use there.

en.lifeSummary: When John James Ellithorpe Watson was born in June 1472, in Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, England, his father, Daniel Watson, was 22 and his mother, Matilda Harriet Henderson, was 23. He married Agnes Young in 1495, in Cleveland, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 11 March 1521, in Low Moor, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 48, and was buried in Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: John James Ellithorpe Watson(이)가 1472년 6월 영국 요르크실르 홀--스팔딩-모어에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Daniel Watson(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Harriet Henderson(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1495년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 클레벨랜드에서 Agnes Young(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1521년 3월 11일 영국 요르크실르 로 무어에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 홀--스팔딩-모어에 묻혔다.

холме-он-спалдинг-мур, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John James Ellithorpe Watson родился июнь 1472 г. в местности холме-он-спалдинг-мур, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Daniel Watson было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Matilda Harriet Henderson, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Young в 1495 г. в местности кливеланд, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 11 марта 1521 г. в местности лоу мур, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 48 лет и был похоронен в местности холме-он-спалдинг-мур, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John James Ellithorpe Watson nació en junio de 1472, en Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Daniel Watson, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Matilda Harriet Henderson, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Agnes Young en 1495, en Cleveland, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 11 de marzo de 1521, en Low Moor, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultado en Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
